Notice Of Hearing On Defendants Petition To Compel Binding Arbitration Regarding Plaintiff Kenley Gaffke
Off Calendar Oct-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN REMO HOTEL, LP, ROBERT FIELD's Petition To Compel Binding Arbitration Regarding Plaintiff Kenley Gaffke.

Defendants San Remo Hotel, LP and Robert Field's petition to compel arbitration of the claims alleged by plaintiff Kenly Gaffke is granted. Moving defendants have shown that the parties entered in a valid enforceable arbitration agreement covering Mr. Gaffke's claims. Although the agreement was not signed by the moving defendants, their conduct showed they considered themselves bound by the agreement. (Serafin v. Balco Properties Ltd., LLC (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 165, 177.) The identity of the parties bound by the agreement can be ascertained by extrinsic evidence and, per Mr. Gaffke's declaration, the intent of the agreement was to bind San Remo Hotel, LP and its owners, among others. Although the arbitration agreement has indicia of procedural unconscionability because it is a contract of adhesion, Mr. Gaffke has failed to show any substantive unconscionability. The discovery provided in Civil Code section 1283.05 protects Mr. Gaffke's ability to vindicate his claims and the agreement impliedly obligates moving defendants to pay for the cost of arbitration, an obligation acknowledged by their counsel. Mr. Gaffke's claims must be resolved by binding arbitration and all proceedings regarding those claims in this case are stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration proceedings.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, Maria.Torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/HK)
